$7.4&nbsp;million in 2010, or $0.05 per share, and foreign exchange losses of $10.9&nbsp;million in 2009, or ($0.09) per share. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Revenue  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Revenue for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011 was $669.1&nbsp;million, which was up 12.5% from 2010. The increase in revenue
resulted from higher sales volumes and higher average realized sales prices. We sold 40.1&nbsp;million pounds of molybdenum in 2011, which was 3.1&nbsp;million pounds more than the volume sold in
2010. The average realized sales price for molybdenum in 2011 was $16.28 per pound, which was $0.61 per pound higher compared to the average realized sales price for molybdenum in 2010. Revenue from
toll roasted material for third parties was $17.2&nbsp;million in 2011, an increase of $1.0&nbsp;million compared to $16.2&nbsp;million in 2010, primarily as a result of higher market demand. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Higher
sales volume in 2011 resulted primarily from a drawdown of mined production from the TC Mine and Endako Mine, and partly from higher sales volume of third-party product that was
purchased and sold. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Revenue
in 2010 was $594.8&nbsp;million, up $221.4&nbsp;million or approximately 59.3% from $373.4&nbsp;million in 2009. Revenue in 2010 increased as a result of higher average
realized sales prices for molybdenum, with an average realized price in 2010 of $15.67 per pound, up approximately 38.9% from $11.28 per pound in 2009. Molybdenum sold from our mines in 2010 was
29.1&nbsp;million pounds, up approximately 6.1% from 27.4&nbsp;million pounds sold in 2009. Sales volumes from third-party product purchased and processed were 7.9&nbsp;million pounds in 2010,
up 67.7% from 4.7&nbsp;million pounds in 2009. Revenue from toll roasted material for third parties was $16.2&nbsp;million in 2010, up approximately 40.9% compared to $11.5&nbsp;million in 2009
primarily as a result of higher volume driven by an increase in commodity prices throughout 2010. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Operating Expenses  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Operating expenses in 2011 were $400.4&nbsp;million, up $84.9&nbsp;million, or 26.9% compared to the same period in 2010. The
increase in operating expenses in 2011 was primarily the result of the waste stripping activities at our TC Mine, higher sales volume, an increase in higher-cost third-party molybdenum
that was purchased, processed, and sold, and lower-of-cost-or-market product inventory write-downs of $24.9&nbsp;million for both mines. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
non-GAAP financial measure of cash cost per pound produced from our mines increased in 2011 to $7.94 per pound from $6.07 per pound in 2010. The increase in the 2011 cash
cost per pound produced was due to lower production and higher costs at the TC Mine in the second half of 2011 associated with the planned mine pit sequencing that resulted in increased waste
stripping activities, lower grade ore and lower mill recovery rates, which were substantially offset by record production and higher ore grades and mill recovery rates at TC Mine in the first half of
2011. In addition, cash cost per pound produced at the Endako Mine was higher in 2011 primarily as a result of higher repairs and maintenance costs to crushing and conveying systems and milling
equipment. Operating expenses at the Endako Mine were also impacted by unfavorable foreign exchange rates converting C$ costs to US$. The foreign exchange rate averaged US$1.00&nbsp;= C$0.99 in 2011
compared to an average rate of US$1.00&nbsp;= C$1.03 in 2010. See "Non-GAAP Financial Measures" below for the calculation and reconciliation of cash cost per pound produced. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Operating
expenses in 2010 were $315.5&nbsp;million, up $74.2&nbsp;million or approximately 30.8% from $241.3&nbsp;million in 2009, primarily due to higher sales volume in 2010.
Prices for third-party molybdenum that was purchased, processed, and sold were up significantly during 2010 compared to 2009, which, </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>61</FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
non-GAAP financial measure of cash cost per pound produced from our mines increased in 2010 to $6.07 per pound from $5.84 per pound in 2009. The increase in the cash cost
per pound produced in 2010 was primarily the result of a higher cash cost per pound produced at the Endako Mine due to higher mining and milling costs. A breakdown of the Endako Mine

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Depreciation, Depletion and Amortization Expense  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Depreciation, depletion and amortization expense in 2011 was $67.1&nbsp;million, an increase of $17.2&nbsp;million, or
approximately 34.5% compared to 2010. The increase in 2011 primarily resulted from higher molybdenum sales from our mines in 2011 and the depreciation of new mining equipment placed into service since
December&nbsp;31, 2010. Product inventory costs include depreciation, depletion and amortization. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Depreciation,
depletion and amortization expense in 2010 was $49.9&nbsp;million or approximately 15.0% more than $43.4&nbsp;million in 2009. The increase in 2010 primarily resulted
from higher molybdenum sales from our mines in 2010. This increase was partially offset by the effects of increased mineral reserve estimates established at the TC Mine late in the third quarter of
2009. Product inventory costs include depreciation, depletion and amortization. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> General and Administrative Expense  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;General and administrative expense in 2011 was $28.3&nbsp;million, compared to $23.5&nbsp;million and $25.1&nbsp;million in 2010,
and 2009, respectively. General and administrative expense in 2011, 2010, and 2009 included $4.8&nbsp;million, $4.1&nbsp;million, and $8.7&nbsp;million, respectively, of non-cash
stock-based compensation expense. The stock-based compensation for 2011 and 2010 was lower than 2009 due to a lower amount of stock-based awards granted together with a lower valuation resulting
primarily from a significantly lower stock price. Additionally, the 2009 stock-based compensation included a non-cash compensation charge of $2.8&nbsp;million related to a voluntary
stock option surrender program. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Excluding
the effects of stock-based compensation, general and administrative expense is higher in 2011 compared to 2010. The increase in general and administrative expense in 2011 was
primarily the result of higher compensation costs associated with additional employees hired as well as increased strategic and other consulting expenses due to our growth activities. Excluding the
effects of stock-based compensation, general and administrative expense in 2010 was higher than 2009 primarily as a result of costs related to first-time SEC proxy filings and higher
compensation costs in 2010. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Acquisition costs  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Acquisition costs of $12.9&nbsp;million in 2010 related to investment banking fees, legal and consulting costs associated with the
Terrane acquisition. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Interest and finance fees of $5.2&nbsp;million in 2011 primarily related to approximately $2.3&nbsp;million of commitment fees on
the unused $300.0&nbsp;million revolving credit facility, $2.1&nbsp;million of debt issuance cost amortization, and $0.8&nbsp;million of interest on the mobile mining equipment loans. In 2011,
we capitalized interest and debt issuance costs totaling $16.8&nbsp;million. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Interest
and finance fees of $0.9&nbsp;million in 2010 primarily related to approximately $0.6&nbsp;million of interest expense on equipment loans, and the amortization of

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Interest
and finance fees of $1.2&nbsp;million in 2009 primarily represented interest expense on equipment loans, together with commitment fees on an unused $35.0&nbsp;million
revolving credit facility. Effective February&nbsp;2, 2010, we terminated the $35.0&nbsp;million revolving credit facility. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Foreign Exchange Gains and Losses  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Foreign exchange loss in 2011 was $13.1&nbsp;million compared to a foreign exchange gain of $7.4&nbsp;million in 2010 and a foreign
exchange loss of $10.9&nbsp;million in 2009. During 2011, the US$ strengthened against the C$, which resulted in an unrealized foreign exchange loss of $11.8&nbsp;million on intercompany notes
that are denominated in a different currency than their measurement currency and an unrealized foreign exchange gain of $1.6&nbsp;million on foreign currency derivative instruments. The remaining
foreign exchange loss was the result of C$ cash balances that have the US$ as their measurement currency and on US$ payables and receivables that have the C$ as their measurement currency. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Foreign
exchange gain in 2010 of $7.4&nbsp;million was primarily the result of a $4.6&nbsp;million foreign exchange gain from the settlement of an intercompany note from Canadian
operations related to the Terrane acquisition. In addition, gains of $2.9&nbsp;million and $1.9&nbsp;million on foreign exchange derivative instruments were recognized in 2010 and 2009,
respectively. </FONT></P>

foreign exchange loss. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
foreign exchange rate was US$1.00&nbsp;= C$1.02 at December&nbsp;31, 2011 compared to US$1.00&nbsp;= C$.99 at December&nbsp;31, 2010 and US$1.00&nbsp;= C$1.05 at
December&nbsp;31, 2009. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Income and Mining Tax Expense (Benefit)  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Income and mining tax expense was $11.2&nbsp;million in 2011, which was $9.0&nbsp;million lower and $9.2&nbsp;million higher than
income and mining tax expense of $20.2&nbsp;million and $2.0&nbsp;million in 2010 and 2009, respectively. Income and mining tax expense for 2011 benefited from lower taxable income, increased
percentage depletion due to higher sales and declining Canadian national and provincial tax rates. Income and mining tax expense for 2010 benefitted from a net refund of $7.1&nbsp;million of certain
state income taxes related to state tax planning; a realization of alternative minimum tax credits in 2010, which resulted in a $2.9&nbsp;million reduction of the related valuation allowance (due to
a tax election related to the treatment of development costs); and an out-of-period adjustment of $4.5&nbsp;million due to a difference between the 2009 income tax provision
and the 2009 tax return, whereby we realized an additional net operating loss carry-back. Income and mining taxes for 2009 benefitted from a re-evaluation of US state income
tax rates, declining Canadian provincial income tax rates and proportionately higher percentage depletion deduction and foreign tax differences primarily due to lower average realized molybdenum
prices. The non-cash unrealized gains and losses on the common stock purchase warrants during the three years did not generate any income tax expenses or benefits. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B> US Operations Molybdenum  </B></FONT></P>

<DIV style="padding:0pt;position:relative;text-align:left;margin-left:10%;">
 <DL compact>
<DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(1)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Mined
molybdenum production pounds reflected are molybdenum oxide and HPM.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(2)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>The
TC Mine cash cost represents the mining (including all stripping costs), milling, mine site administration, roasting and packaging for molybdenum oxide
and HPM produced in the period. Cash cost excludes: the effect of purchase price adjustments, the effects of changes in inventory, corporate allocations, stock-based compensation and other
non-cash employee benefits and depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion. The cash cost for the TC Mine includes an </FONT></DD></DL>
 </DIV>
 <P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>64</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>
<!-- ZEQ.=7,SEQ=64,EFW="2207269",CP="THOMPSON CREEK METALS COMPANY IN",DN="1",CHK=231903,FOLIO='64',FILE='DISK129:[12ZAB1.12ZAB13501]DQ13501A.;12',USER='DTAYLOR',CD='27-FEB-2012;14:36' -->
<A NAME="page_dq13501_1_65"> </A>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>
 <DIV style="padding:0pt;position:relative;text-align:left;margin-left:10%;">
<UL>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>estimated
molybdenum loss (sulfide to oxide), transportation costs, and an allocation of roasting and packaging costs from the Langeloth Facility. See "Non-GAAP Financial Measures" for
additional information. </FONT></P>

</UL>
 </DIV>
 <UL>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><I> Molybdenum Production and Cash Costs  </I></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The TC Mine had record molybdenum production in 2010 and the first half of 2011 due to the planned mine pit sequencing and the mining
of high grade ore primarily in the bottom of the pit during these periods. In 2011, the TC Mine produced 21.4&nbsp;million pounds, down approximately 14.8% from a record 25.1&nbsp;million pounds
produced during 2010. The overall lower production for 2011 was primarily the result of significantly lower production in the second half of 2011 due to the lower-grade ore and a lower mill recovery
rate associated with the planned mine pit sequencing, which somewhat offset record production in the first half of 2011. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Molybdenum
production from TC Mine was 2.5&nbsp;million pounds in the fourth quarter of 2011, which was down 66.9% compared to the fourth quarter of 2010. Lower production was
primarily the result of a significantly lower ore-grade and mill recovery rate due to the mine pit sequencing, as discussed above. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Production
in 2010 was a record high of 25.1&nbsp;million pounds compared to 2009 production of 17.8&nbsp;million pounds primarily due to higher tons mined and milled and higher ore
grade in 2010. Lower production in 2009 also was partly impacted by a planned 2-week shutdown in July 2009. Higher tons mined and milled in 2010 compared to 2009 was the result of the
modified 2009 work schedule. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
non-GAAP financial measure of cash cost per pound produced increased to $13.05 per pound in the fourth quarter of 2011 from $4.72 per pound in the fourth quarter of 2010.
See "Non-GAAP Financial Measures" below for the definition and calculation of cash cost per pound produced. The increase in the 2011 fourth quarter cash cost per pound compared to the
fourth quarter of 2010 was primarily the result of costs related to increased waste stripping activities together with lower production in the fourth quarter of 2011 as explained above. The cash costs
in the fourth quarter of 2011 included stripping costs of $10.07&nbsp;million, or $ 3.99 per pound produced, compared to stripping costs of $7.6&nbsp;million, or $1.00 per pound produced in the
fourth quarter of 2010. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
cash cost per pound produced in 2011 increased 28.1% to $6.66 per pound compared to $5.20 per pound in 2010 primarily due to lower production and higher costs in the second half of
2011 associated with the planned mine pit sequencing, increased waste stripping activities and the lower grade ore and mill recovery rates, which was substantially offset by record production and
higher ore grades and mill recovery rates at the TC Mine in the first half of 2011. The cash costs in 2011 included stripping costs of $53.3&nbsp;million, or $ 2.50 per pound produced, compared to
stripping costs of $26.0&nbsp;million, or $1.04 per pound produced in 2010. See "Non-GAAP Financial Measures" below for the definition and calculation of cash cost per pound produced. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
cash cost per pound produced in 2010 decreased 9.1% to $5.20 per pound compared to $5.72 per pound in 2009 primarily due to record production in 2010. The cash costs in 2010 included
stripping costs of $26.0&nbsp;million, or $1.04 per pound produced, compared to stripping costs of $26.1&nbsp;million, or $1.47 per pound produced in 2009. The impact of higher production in 2010
was partly offset by higher repairs and maintenance costs, higher fuel costs and higher employee compensation costs. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><I> Molybdenum sold  </I></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Molybdenum sold from the TC Mine material in the fourth quarter of 2011 of 4.0&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price
of $13.94 per pound was down approximately 34.0% from 6.1&nbsp;million pounds sold at an average realized sales price of $16.07 per pound in the fourth quarter of 2010. </FONT></P>

</UL>
<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>65</FONT></P>

<UL>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>During
the fourth quarter of 2011, sales included deliveries under certain forward sales contracts of approximately 0.5&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of $9.70 per pound
compared to approximately 0.3&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of $10.92 per pound in the fourth quarter of 2010. All deliveries under the forward sales contracts have now been
completed. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For
2011, TC Mine sold 25.5&nbsp;million pounds of molybdenum at an average realized sales price of $16.42 per pound, compared to 22.3&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized
sales price of $15.67 per pound in 2010. During 2011, sales included deliveries under certain forward sales contracts of approximately 1.9&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of
$10.51 per pound compared to approximately 1.5&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of $10.53 per pound for 2010. All deliveries under these forward sales contracts have now been
completed. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In
2010, TC Mine sold 22.3&nbsp;million pounds of molybdenum compared to 19.4&nbsp;million pounds sold in 2009. During 2010, approximately 1.5&nbsp;million pounds at an average
realized sales price of $10.53 per pound were sold under certain forward sales contracts, compared to approximately 1.9&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of $8.42 per pound in
2009. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Langeloth Facility  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Operating results for the Langeloth Facility represent activities related to the roasting and processing of third-party concentrate and
other metals at the Langeloth Facility and excludes product volumes and costs related to the roasting and processing of TC Mine and Endako Mine product. Langeloth Facility costs associated with
roasting and processing of TC Mine and Endako Mine product are included in their respective operating results. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
following is a summary of the Langeloth Facility's operating results for the three months ended December&nbsp;31, 2011 and 2010, and years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2010, and
2009: </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In
the fourth quarter of 2011, molybdenum sold from third-party purchased molybdenum concentrate was up 39.8% over the fourth quarter of 2010 and was sold at an average realized sales
price that was approximately 11.4% lower than the fourth quarter of 2010. Third-party concentrate purchases and sales volumes increased in the current quarter compared to the same quarter of 2010,
primarily due to lower production from the TC Mine in the second half of 2011. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Molybdenum
sold from purchased product of 8.2&nbsp;million pounds for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, was up 5.0% from the same period in 2010. The average realized sales
price on molybdenum sold from purchased product was $15.95 per pound in 2011 compared to $15.75 for the same period in 2010. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
volume of toll roasted molybdenum processed during the fourth quarter of 2011 and the full year 2011 increased approximately 2.5% and 24.0%, respectively, compared to the same
periods of 2010 primarily as a result of increased customer demand. </FONT></P>

<DIV style="padding:0pt;position:relative;text-align:left;margin-left:10%;">
 <DL compact>
<DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(1)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Mined
molybdenum production pounds are molybdenum oxide.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(2)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>The
Endako Mine cash cost represents the mining (including all stripping costs), milling, mine site administration, roasting and packaging for molybdenum
oxide produced in the period. Cash cost excludes: the effect of purchase price adjustments, the effects of changes in inventory, corporate allocations, stock-based compensation and other
non-cash employee benefits, and depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion. See "Non-GAAP Financial Measures" for additional information. </FONT></DD></DL>
 </DIV>
 <P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><I> Molybdenum Production and Cash Costs  </I></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our 75% share of molybdenum production at the Endako Mine increased 5.8% in the fourth quarter of 2011 to 1.8&nbsp;million pounds
from 1.7&nbsp;million pounds in the fourth quarter of 2010. This increase was primarily due to higher tons mined and milled partly offset by lower ore grade and mill recovery rate in the fourth
quarter of 2011. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For
2011, our 75% share of molybdenum production at the Endako Mine decreased 7.0% to 7.0&nbsp;million pounds compared to 7.5&nbsp;million pounds in 2010. This decrease was primarily
due to lower ore grade and a lower mill recovery rate in 2011 compared to 2010, partly offset by higher tons mined and milled. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our
75% share of molybdenum production at the Endako Mine in 2010 increased slightly to 7.5&nbsp;million pounds compared to 7.4&nbsp;million pounds produced in 2009. The slight
increase in production in 2010 was primarily due to higher ore tons mined and milled, which was offset by a lower mill recovery rate. For the first half of 2009, the mine and mill were operating at
80% of capacity as planned due to weak economic conditions. Additionally, 2009 production was reduced due to a planned 2-week shutdown in July 2009. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
non-GAAP financial measure of cash cost per pound produced increased to $12.19 per pound in the fourth quarter of 2011 from $10.65 per pound in the fourth quarter of
2010. See "Non-GAAP Financial Measures" below for the definition and reconciliation of cash cost per pound produced. The cash costs for the Endako Mine were impacted by higher mining costs
primarily due to repairs and </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>67</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>
<!-- ZEQ.=1,SEQ=67,EFW="2207269",CP="THOMPSON CREEK METALS COMPANY IN",DN="1",CHK=1029443,FOLIO='67',FILE='DISK129:[12ZAB1.12ZAB13501]DS13501A.;15',USER='DTAYLOR',CD='27-FEB-2012;11:18' -->
<A NAME="page_ds13501_1_68"> </A>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>maintenance
on crushing and conveyor systems, mill equipment repairs, and a write-down of surplus and obsolete materials and supplies inventory for the existing mill. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For
2011, the cash cost per pound produced increased to $11.86 per pound compared to $8.89 per pound in 2010. The increase in cash cost per pound produced in 2011 was primarily the
result of higher repairs and maintenance costs to crushing and conveying systems, which also resulted in higher ore haulage costs and mill equipment repairs. Additionally, cold and wet weather
conditions caused higher than expected downtime in the first half of 2011 due to material getting caught in chutes and plugging up screening systems. Costs were also impacted by unfavorable foreign
exchange rates converting C$ costs to US$ costs. The foreign exchange rate averaged US$1.00&nbsp;= C$0.99 in 2011 compared to an average rate of US$1.00&nbsp;= C$1.03 in 2010. A $0.01 change in
the Canadian foreign exchange rate results in a change in the cash cost per pound produced of approximately $0.10 per pound. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For
2010, the cash cost per pound produced increased to $8.89 per pound compared to $6.13 per pound in 2009. The increase in cash cost per pound produced in 2010 was primarily the result
of higher mining and milling costs due to a breakdown of the in-pit conveyor system, which resulted in higher truck haulage costs, and higher repairs and maintenance costs in the mill.
Costs were also impacted by unfavorable foreign exchange rates converting C$ costs to US$ costs. The foreign exchange rate averaged US$1.00&nbsp;= C$1.03 in 2010 compared to an average rate of
US$1.00&nbsp;= C$1.14 in 2009. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><I> Molybdenum sold  </I></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our share of molybdenum sold from the Endako Mine in the fourth quarter of 2011 was 1.4&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized
sales price of $14.06 per pound compared to 1.5&nbsp;million pounds sold at an average realized sales price of $15.83 per pound in the fourth quarter of 2010. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For
2011, our share of molybdenum sold from the Endako Mine was 6.3&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of $16.12 per pound compared to 6.8&nbsp;million pounds at
an average realized sales price of $15.56 per pound in 2010. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For
2010, our share of molybdenum sold from the Endako Mine was 6.8&nbsp;million pounds at an average realized sales price of $15.56 per pound compared to 8.0&nbsp;million pounds
sold at an average realized
sales price of $11.37 per pound in 2009. The decrease in pounds sold in 2010 resulted from higher sales in 2009 associated with a drawdown of product inventory. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><I> Mill expansion project  </I></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;During the three and twelve months ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, we incurred C$49.4&nbsp;million and C$213.9&nbsp;million,
respectively, representing our share of capital expenditures for the mill expansion project, excluding capitalized interest and debt issuance costs. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Total
capital expenditures for the Endako mill expansion project are estimated at approximately C$650.0&nbsp;million&nbsp;- C$655.0&nbsp;million (100% basis), including
start-up and commissioning costs. Commissioning of the new Endako mill is now complete and commercial production was achieved on February&nbsp;1, 2012. The construction of the regrind
circuit for the new mill is still in process and is expected to be completed by the end of the first quarter of 2012. In the interim, we continue to use the cleaner and regrind circuit in the existing
mill. Since inception of the project through December&nbsp;31, 2011, approximately C$593.4&nbsp;million has been spent, of which our share is approximately C$450.7&nbsp;million. In December
2011, we entered into an agreement with Sojitz pursuant to which the parties agreed that Sojitz will pay 12.5% of the cost of the Endako Expansion Project between C$548.24&nbsp;million and
C$650.0&nbsp;million and that its 25% interest in the Endako Mine would remain unchanged. If the cost of the Endako expansion project exceeds C$650.0&nbsp;million, Sojitz agreed it would fund 25%
of such excess up to C$675.0&nbsp;million, and its share of any amounts above C$675.0&nbsp;million would need to be negotiated. This agreement settled a dispute between the parties regarding the </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>68</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>
<!-- ZEQ.=2,SEQ=68,EFW="2207269",CP="THOMPSON CREEK METALS COMPANY IN",DN="1",CHK=88911,FOLIO='68',FILE='DISK129:[12ZAB1.12ZAB13501]DS13501A.;15',USER='DTAYLOR',CD='27-FEB-2012;11:18' -->
<A NAME="page_ds13501_1_69"> </A>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>parties'
respective obligations to fund the total capital cost of the project. Therefore, based upon the final estimated costs of C$650.0&nbsp;million&nbsp;- C$655.0&nbsp;million (100% basis),
including start-up and commissioning costs, our share of the expansion project capital expenditures are expected to be approximately C$500.0&nbsp;million&nbsp;- C$504.0&nbsp;million. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B> Copper-Gold (Development)  </B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Mt. Milligan Project  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;During the fourth quarter and year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, we incurred C$191.9&nbsp;million and C$519.0&nbsp;million,
respectively, of capital expenditures for the Mt. Milligan project, excluding capitalized interest and debt issuance costs. Capital expenditures were primarily related to construction activities
related to the tailing storage facility, plant site earthworks, cement works, steel erection, and engineering design costs. Since inception of the project C$575.9&nbsp;million has been spent through
December&nbsp;31, 2011, including approximately C$40.9&nbsp;million spent prior to the acquisition of Terrane. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Given
industry wide cost escalation challenges resulting from scarcity of inputs and resources, the cost to complete the Mt.&nbsp;Milligan project may be 10% to 20% higher than our
previous estimate, depending upon weather conditions, labor availability and productivity, the timing of equipment deliveries and any unforeseen delays. The Company is currently estimating
approximately C$1.4 to C$1.5&nbsp;billion of total capital to construct and develop the Mt.&nbsp;Milligan copper-gold mine. We continue to monitor our current costs, future cost estimates and
scheduling for the project and will revise the estimate upon future developments. There can be no assurance that our current capital estimate and project completion schedule will be achieved. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B> Liquidity and Capital Resources  </B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At December&nbsp;31, 2011, we had cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments of $294.5&nbsp;million compared to
$316.0&nbsp;million at December&nbsp;31, 2010. We monitor our positions with, and the credit quality of, the financial institutions and companies in which we invests our cash, cash equivalents,
and short-term investments. Our investment policy limits investments to government-backed financial instruments, commercial paper and other investments meeting the guidelines of our
investment policy. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We
manage our credit risk from accounts receivable through our collection activities. As of the date of this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K, we have not experienced any
material delinquencies regarding the collection of our accounts receivable. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At
December&nbsp;31, 2011, we had working capital of $283.5&nbsp;million, including $294.5&nbsp;million of cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments,
$78.6&nbsp;million of receivables, and $6.7&nbsp;million of short-term debt, including capital lease obligations, primarily related to equipment financings. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Gold Stream Arrangement  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In December 2011, we entered into an amended and restated gold stream agreement with Royal Gold giving them the right to purchase an
additional 15% interest in the refined gold production from Mt. Milligan for $270.0&nbsp;million, plus $435 per ounce, or prevailing market rate, if lower than $435, when the gold is delivered. When
we purchased Terrane in 2010, we entered into a similar transaction with Royal Gold giving them the right to purchase 25% of the refined gold production from Mt. Milligan for $311.5&nbsp;million.
Payments made by Royal Gold upon signing the original and amended and restated agreements were $252.6&nbsp;million and $112.0&nbsp;million, respectively. Royal Gold will make the remaining future
scheduled payments to us in 2012 and 2013 in the aggregate amount of $216.9&nbsp;million. </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>69</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>

<A NAME="page_ds13501_1_70"> </A>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> 7.375% Senior Unsecured Notes  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On May&nbsp;20, 2011, we issued $350.0&nbsp;million 7.375% senior unsecured notes (the "Notes"). The proceeds received in the
offering were $339.9&nbsp;million, which were net of financing fees of $10.1&nbsp;million. The net proceeds from the Notes offering have been and are expected to be used to fund the development of
Mt. Milligan and for general working capital purposes. The Notes are redeemable at our option, in whole or in part, at any time on or after June&nbsp;1, 2014. The Notes mature on June&nbsp;1, 2018
and accrue interest from May&nbsp;20, 2011 until maturity at a fixed rate of 7.375% per year. Interest is payable in cash semi-annually in arrears on June&nbsp;1 and December&nbsp;1,
and the first interest payment occurred on December&nbsp;1, 2011. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
Notes are guaranteed on a senior basis by substantially all of our subsidiaries. The Notes include both standard financial and non-financial covenants, including, among
others, limitations on incurring additional indebtedness, making restricted payments, and allowing new liens. As of December&nbsp;31, 2011, we were in compliance with these covenants. In connection
with the issuance of the Notes, we as guarantors, and the initial purchasers, entered into an agreement obligating us to file&nbsp;a registration statement with the SEC so that the holders of the
Notes can exchange the Notes for registered notes and related guarantees evidencing the same indebtedness as the Notes. In December 2011, we completed the exchange offer of the original Notes for a
like principal amount of exchange notes registered under the Securities Act of 1933. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Credit facility  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We have in place a senior secured revolving credit agreement (the "Credit Agreement"). On February&nbsp;24, 2011, we entered into the
First Amendment to the Credit Agreement, which increased the facility from $290.0&nbsp;million to $300.0&nbsp;million. On May&nbsp;20, 2011, and concurrent with the offering of the Notes (more
fully described above), we entered into the Second Amendment to the Credit Agreement to, among other things, allow for the issuance of the Notes. Subsequently, on December&nbsp;13, 2011, and
concurrent with the amended and restated purchase and sale agreement with Royal Gold (as more fully described above), we entered into the Third Amendment to the Credit Agreement to, among other
things, allow for the amended and restated purchase and sale agreement with Royal Gold and to amend certain financial covenants and non-financial covenants, including ratio tests for
leverage and interest coverage, as well as the liquidity test to be met at the time of any drawdown. Our obligations under the Credit Agreement are secured by a senior lien on substantially all of our
tangible and intangible assets. As of December&nbsp;31, 2011, we were in compliance with the Credit Agreement's covenants. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As
of December&nbsp;31, 2011, we had no outstanding borrowings and had issued and outstanding $9.0&nbsp;million in letters of credit under the Credit Agreement. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Caterpillar equipment financing facility  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On March&nbsp;30, 2011, we entered into a Master Funding and Lease Agreement with Caterpillar Financial Services Limited
("Caterpillar"), which was amended and restated on December&nbsp;9, 2011 (the "Master Agreement"). The Master Agreement provides for up to $132.0&nbsp;million in equipment financings comprised of
three separate tranches of $20.0&nbsp;million, $50.0&nbsp;million, and $62.0&nbsp;million. The $20.0&nbsp;million tranche is currently available, and the $50.0&nbsp;million and
$62.0&nbsp;million tranches are available upon the satisfaction of certain conditions specified in the Master Agreement. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We
entered into the Master Agreement to finance our purchases of mobile mining equipment for use at the Mt. Milligan project. Pursuant to the Master Agreement and agreements entered into
in connection therewith, we may draw down on the facility and use the proceeds from each drawdown to fund the purchase of this equipment. Caterpillar will purchase such equipment and simultaneously
lease the equipment to us. </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>70</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>
<!-- ZEQ.=4,SEQ=70,EFW="2207269",CP="THOMPSON CREEK METALS COMPANY IN",DN="1",CHK=772174,FOLIO='70',FILE='DISK129:[12ZAB1.12ZAB13501]DS13501A.;15',USER='DTAYLOR',CD='27-FEB-2012;11:18' -->
<A NAME="page_ds13501_1_71"> </A>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each
borrowing under the facility will be for a term of 60&nbsp;months. We will pay interest on the amounts borrowed under the facility at either floating or fixed rates, at our
option, calculated as set forth in the Master Agreement. Our ability to request advances under the facility will terminate 33&nbsp;months following its effective date (or such later date as may be
agreed upon by Caterpillar), and any unused commitments under the facility will then terminate and no longer be available to us. At the end of each 60-month lease period, we have the
option to purchase the underlying equipment for a nominal sum. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
Master Agreement contains various affirmative and negative covenants and customary events of default. Under the terms of the Master Agreement, we are required to be in compliance
with the consolidated leverage ratio and consolidated interest coverage ratio financial covenants. In addition, as a condition to any drawdown under the facility, we must be in compliance with the
consolidated liquidity financial covenant included in our Credit Agreement. As of December&nbsp;31, 2011, we were in compliance with the Credit Agreement's covenants. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As
of December&nbsp;31, 2011, we had drawn down approximately $8.2&nbsp;million under the first tranche of the equipment financing facility. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Operating Cash Flows  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash generated by operating activities in 2011 was $202.7&nbsp;million compared to $157.4&nbsp;million in 2010. This increase in
cash flow from operations was primarily the result of changes in working capital mainly due to favorable year-end Canada to US exchange rates and an increase in accounts payable. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash
generated by operating activities in 2010 was $157.4&nbsp;million compared to $105.9&nbsp;million in 2009. This increase in cash flow from operations was primarily the result of
increased operating income resulting from higher molybdenum sales volumes and prices in 2010 compared to 2009, partly offset by unfavorable working capital changes mainly related to higher inventory
and accounts receivable balances. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Investing Activities  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash used in investing activities in 2011 was $716.5&nbsp;million compared to cash used of $242.6&nbsp;million in 2010. During
2011, we spent $686.7&nbsp;million on property, plant, and equipment expenditures, primarily related to the Endako Mine mill expansion and the development of Mt. Milligan. These 2011 capital
expenditures excluded $111.2&nbsp;million of accrued costs at December&nbsp;31, 2011. Additionally, cash used in investing activities for 2011 included $16.0&nbsp;million of restricted cash,
primarily comprised of amounts withheld related to construction holdback accounts related to the Endako Mine mill expansion and the development of Mt. Milligan and $13.7&nbsp;million of interest
paid on the Notes, which was capitalized. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash
used in investing activities in 2010 was $242.6&nbsp;million compared to $412.6&nbsp;million used in 2009. During 2010, we received $355.2&nbsp;million from maturities of
short-term investments, which provided a significant portion of the $383.4&nbsp;million net cash outlay for the acquisition of Terrane. Additionally, investing activities in 2010
included $213.7&nbsp;million of property, plant, and equipment expenditures primarily for our 75% share of the mill expansion project at the Endako Mine and operating capital at our TC Mine, Endako
Mine and Langeloth Facility. These 2010 capital expenditures excluded $32.5&nbsp;million of accrued costs at December&nbsp;31, 2010. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In
2009, we made short-term investments of $341.3&nbsp;million together with $66.1&nbsp;million of plant, property and equipment expenditures. These
short-term investments consisted of US and Canadian government-backed securities with maturities of greater than 90&nbsp;days but less than 180&nbsp;days. The increase in property,
plant, and equipment expenditures in 2010 compared to 2009 was primarily due to </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>71</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>the
mill expansion project at the Endako Mine together with the purchase of additional mining equipment at both the TC Mine and Endako Mine. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Financing Activities  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash generated by financing activities in 2011 was $495.9&nbsp;million compared to cash generated of $236.0&nbsp;million in 2010.
This increase was primarily related to net proceeds of
$339.9 from the issuance of Notes in May 2011, $138.1&nbsp;million of proceeds received from Royal Gold under the Gold Stream Arrangement, and $26.4&nbsp;million of net proceeds from the exercise
of stock options. The proceeds were partially offset by $5.4&nbsp;million of debt repayments under the equipment financings and $3.1&nbsp;million of debt issuance costs. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In
2010, TCM received $226.5&nbsp;million of proceeds from the gold stream transaction upon closing of the acquisition of Terrane, together with $9.1&nbsp;million of net proceeds
from equipment financings and $7.6&nbsp;million of net proceeds from the exercise of stock options, which were somewhat offset by $7.2&nbsp;million of debt issuance costs for the
$290.0&nbsp;million revolving credit facility that was closed in the fourth quarter of 2010. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In
2009, TCM received net proceeds of $194.6&nbsp;million related to the issuance of 15.5&nbsp;million shares and $11.4&nbsp;million related to stock option exercises, which more
than offset scheduled principal payments on equipment loans of $5.3&nbsp;million. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Liquidity and Capital Resources Summary  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At December&nbsp;31, 2011, we have estimated future cash capital project expenditures of approximately $1.02&nbsp;billion to
$1.16&nbsp;billion to complete the Endako mill expansion and the Mt. Milligan mine. We have solidified our ability to finance these capital project expenditures with the $294.5&nbsp;million of
consolidated cash on hand, the remaining proceeds from Royal Gold of $216.9&nbsp;million, $123.8&nbsp;million of undrawn capacity under an equipment lease financing for the mobile mining fleet at
Mt. Milligan, a $291.0&nbsp;million undrawn revolving credit facility and our ongoing cash flow from operations. </FONT></P>

<DL compact>
<DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(3)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Mining
operations are subject to extensive environmental regulations in the jurisdictions in which they operate. Pursuant to environmental regulations, we
are required to close our operations and reclaim and remediate the lands that operations have disturbed. The estimated undiscounted cash outflows of these remediation and reclamation obligations are
reflected here. For more information regarding asset retirement obligations, see Note&nbsp;12 to the Consolidated Financial Statements.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(4)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Purchase
obligations are not recorded in the Consolidated Financial Statements. Purchase obligations represent purchase commitments for the mill expansion
project at the Endako Mine, as well as the Mt. Milligan project. See Note&nbsp;16 to the Consolidated Financial Statements for more information.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(5)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Amount
represents total purchase and sale gold stream transaction. See Note&nbsp;11 to the Consolidated Financial Statements for more information.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(6)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Other
contractual obligations include labor and service contracts. See Note&nbsp;13 to the Consolidated Financial Statements for more information.
Payments related to derivative contracts cannot be reasonably estimated given variable market conditions. See Note&nbsp;7 to the Consolidated Financial Statements for more information. </FONT></DD></DL>
 </DIV>
 <P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B> Off-Balance Sheet Arrangements  </B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As of December&nbsp;31, 2011, we have commitments to purchase approximately 20.5&nbsp;million pounds of molybdenum sulfide
concentrate from 2012 to 2014 to be priced at the time of purchase. In addition, we have fixed-priced contracts, under which we have committed to sell approximately 15,000 pounds of molybdenum during
2012 at an average market price of $19.14 per pound. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On
December&nbsp;9, 2009, we entered into a credit support agreement with British Columbia Hydro and Power Authority ("BC Hydro") related to the mill expansion project at the Endako
Mine. Under this agreement, we are required to post financial assurance in an amount equal to BC Hydro's estimated out-of-pocket costs for work on the expansion project. The
amount of the guarantee as of December&nbsp;31, 2011 was C$16.5&nbsp;million. As part of the financial guarantee, we provided a surety bond for C$11.2&nbsp;million for additional financial
assurance to BC Hydro. The surety bond can be drawn down in the event of a shortfall in BC Hydro's incremental revenues after the commissioning of the new Endako mill facility. At this time, we do not
anticipate having to post any additional financial assurance with respect to the BC Hydro credit support agreement. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As
of December&nbsp;31, 2011, a shortfall in Endako's future electric power usage that would result in incremental payments to BC Hydro cannot be determined and is not deemed to be
probable. As such, no accrual has been recorded. An accrual for any expected shortfall will be recorded if and when it is determined that a shortfall is probable and a reasonable estimate can be made. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B> Non-GAAP Financial Measures  </B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In addition to the consolidated financial statements presented in accordance with US&nbsp;GAAP, we use certain non-GAAP
financial measures of its financial performance for the reasons described further below. The presentation of these measures is not intended to be considered in isolation from, as a substitute for, or
as superior to, the financial information prepared and presented in accordance with US&nbsp;GAAP, and the presentation of these measures may be different from non-GAAP financial measures
used by other
companies. In addition, these non-GAAP measures have limitations in that they do not reflect all of the amounts associated with the results of operations as determined in accordance with
US&nbsp;GAAP. </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>73</FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Adjusted Net Income (Loss), Adjusted Net Income (Loss) Per Share&#151;Basic and Diluted  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adjusted net income (loss), and adjusted net income (loss) per share&#151;basic and diluted, are referred to in this MD&amp;A. These
are considered key measures by our management in evaluating our operating performance on a quarterly and annual basis. Management uses this measure in evaluating our performance as it represents a
profitability measure that is not impacted by changes in the market price of our warrants. These measures do not have standard meanings prescribed by US&nbsp;GAAP and may not be comparable to
similar measures presented by other companies. Management believes these measures provide useful supplemental information to investors in order for them to evaluate our financial performance using the
same measures as management. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adjusted
net income (loss) represents the net income (loss) prepared in accordance with US&nbsp;GAAP, adjusted for significant non-cash items. For 2011, 2010, and 2009, the
significant non-cash items were the non-cash gains and losses on the fair value adjustment related to our outstanding common stock purchase warrants. </FONT></P>


<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We
follow the guidance issued by the Emerging Issues Task Force ("EITF") that common stock purchase warrants with a strike price denominated in a currency other than the entity's
reporting currency are not considered linked to equity, and therefore ,are to be accounted for as derivatives. As a result, our outstanding common stock purchase warrants are accounted for as
derivatives. We recorded a cumulative adjustment to retained earnings upon adoption, and subsequent changes to the fair value of the Warrants are recorded to the consolidated statements of operations
at each quarter-end. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On
October&nbsp;24, 2011, certain of our warrants expired unexercised as our stock price was below the C$9.00 exercise price for such warrants. As a result, after October&nbsp;24,
2011, we will not recognize any non-cash unrealized gains and losses on these warrants. The 2012 Terrane warrants remain outstanding, and we will continue to record unrealized gains and
losses on those warrants until they are exercised or expire on June&nbsp;21, 2012. Other than the consideration pursuant to the
arrangement related to the 2012 Terrane warrants, a cash payment will not be required at the settlement of our warrants; therefore, we do not consider gains or losses on the warrants in the evaluation
of our financial performance. </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>74</FONT></P>

 <P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><B><I> Cash Cost per Pound Produced, Weighted Average Cash Cost per Pound Produced, and Average Realized Sales Price per Pound Sold  </I></B></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash cost per pound produced, weighted average cash cost per pound produced and average realized sales price per pound sold are
considered key measures in evaluating our operating performance. Cash cost per pound produced, weighted average cash cost per pound produced and average realized sales price per pound sold are not
measures of financial performance, nor do they have a standardized meaning prescribed by US&nbsp;GAAP and may not be comparable to similar measures presented by other companies. We use these
measures to evaluate the operating performance at each of our mines, as well as on a consolidated basis, as a measure of profitability and efficiency. We believe that these non-GAAP
measures provide useful supplemental information to investors in order that they may evaluate our performance using the same measures as management and, as a result, the investor is afforded greater
transparency in assessing our financial performance. Non-GAAP financial measures should not be considered in isolation from, as a substitute for, or superior to, measures of financial
performance prepared in accordance with US&nbsp;GAAP. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cash
cost per pound produced represents the mining (including all stripping costs), milling, mine site administration, roasting and packaging costs for molybdenum oxide and HPM produced
at each mine in the period. Stripping costs represent the costs associated with the activity of removing overburden and other mine waste materials in the production phase of a mining operation.
Stripping costs that provide access to mineral reserves that will be produced in future periods are expensed under US&nbsp;GAAP as incurred. Cash cost per pound produced excludes the effects of
purchase price adjustments, the effects of changes in inventory, corporate allocations, stock-based compensation, other non-cash employee benefits and depreciation, depletion, amortization
and accretion. Cash cost for the TC Mine, which only produces molybdenum sulfide and HPM on site, includes an estimated molybdenum loss (sulfide to oxide), an allocation of roasting and packaging
costs from the Langeloth </FONT></P>

<P ALIGN="CENTER" style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>78</FONT></P>

<HR NOSHADE>
<P style='font-family:times;page-break-before:always'></p>
<!-- ZEQ.=4,SEQ=78,EFW="2207269",CP="THOMPSON CREEK METALS COMPANY IN",DN="1",CHK=973173,FOLIO='78',FILE='DISK129:[12ZAB1.12ZAB13501]DU13501A.;12',USER='DTAYLOR',CD='27-FEB-2012;11:01' -->
<A NAME="page_du13501_1_79"> </A>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2><A
HREF="#bg13501a_main_toc">Table of Contents</A></FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Facility
and transportation costs from the TC Mine to the Langeloth Facility. The weighted average cash cost per pound produced represents the cumulative total of the cash costs for the TC Mine and
the Endako Mine divided by the cumulative total production from the TC Mine and the Endako Mine. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
average realized sales price per pound sold represents molybdenum sales revenue divided by the pounds sold. </FONT></P>

<P style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The
following table provides a reconciliation of cash costs and cash cost per pound produced, by mine, and operating expenses included in our consolidated statements of income in the
determination of net income (loss): </FONT></P>

 </DIV>
<DIV style="padding:0pt;position:relative;text-align:left;margin-left:10%;">
 <DL compact>
<DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(1)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Mined
production pounds are molybdenum oxide and HPM from our share of the production from the mines; excludes molybdenum processed from purchased product.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(2)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Cash
costs represent the mining (including all stripping costs), milling, mine site administration, roasting and packaging costs for molybdenum oxide and
HPM produced in the period. Cash cost excludes: the effect of purchase price adjustments, the effects of changes in inventory, corporate allocations, stock-based compensation, other
non-cash employee benefits and depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion. The cash cost for the TC Mine, which only produces molybdenum sulfide and HPM on site, includes an
estimated molybdenum loss (sulfide to oxide), an allocation of roasting and packaging costs from the Langeloth Facility, and transportation costs from the TC Mine to the Langeloth Facility.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(3)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Other
operations represent activities related to the roasting and processing of third-party concentrate and other metals at the Langeloth Facility and
exclude product volumes and costs related to the roasting and processing of TC Mine and Endako Mine concentrate. The Langeloth </FONT></DD></DL>
 </DIV>

<!-- COMMAND=ADD_LINERULETXT,NOSHADE  COLOR="#000000" SIZE="1.0PT" WIDTH="26%" ALIGN="LEFT" -->
<HR NOSHADE  COLOR="#000000" SIZE="1.0PT" WIDTH="26%" ALIGN="LEFT" >
 </DIV>
<DIV style="padding:0pt;position:relative;text-align:left;margin-left:10%;">
 <DL compact>
<DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(1)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Mined
production pounds are molybdenum oxide and HPM from our share of the production from the mines; excludes molybdenum processed from purchased product.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(2)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Cash
costs represent the mining (including all stripping costs), milling, mine site administration, roasting and packaging costs for molybdenum oxide and
HPM produced in the period. Cash cost excludes: the effect of purchase price adjustments, the effects of changes in inventory, corporate allocations, stock-based compensation, other
non-cash employee benefits and depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion. The cash cost for the TC Mine, which only produces molybdenum sulfide and HPM on site, includes an
estimated molybdenum loss (sulfide to oxide), an allocation of roasting and packaging costs from the Langeloth Facility, and transportation costs from the TC Mine to the Langeloth Facility.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(3)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Other
operations represent activities related to the roasting and processing of third-party concentrate and other metals at the Langeloth Facility and
exclude product volumes and costs related to the roasting and processing of TC Mine and Endako Mine concentrate. The Langeloth Facility costs associated with roasting and processing of TC Mine and
Endako Mine concentrate are included in their respective operating results above. </FONT></DD></DL>
 </DIV>

<!-- COMMAND=ADD_LINERULETXT,NOSHADE  COLOR="#000000" SIZE="1.0PT" WIDTH="26%" ALIGN="LEFT" -->
<HR NOSHADE  COLOR="#000000" SIZE="1.0PT" WIDTH="26%" ALIGN="LEFT" >
 </DIV>
<DIV style="padding:0pt;position:relative;text-align:left;margin-left:10%;">
 <DL compact>
<DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(1)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Mined
production pounds are molybdenum oxide and HPM from our share of the production from the mines; excludes molybdenum processed from purchased product.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(2)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Cash
costs represent the mining (including all stripping costs), milling, mine site administration, roasting and packaging costs for molybdenum oxide and
HPM produced in the period. Cash cost excludes: the effect of purchase price adjustments, the effects of changes in inventory, corporate allocations, stock-based compensation, other
non-cash employee benefits and depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion. The cash cost for the TC Mine, which only produces molybdenum sulfide and HPM on site, includes an
estimated molybdenum loss (sulfide to oxide), an allocation of roasting and packaging costs from the Langeloth Facility, and transportation costs from the TC Mine to the Langeloth Facility.
<BR><BR></FONT></DD><DT style='font-family:times;margin-bottom:-11pt;'><FONT SIZE=2>(3)</FONT></DT><DD style="font-family:times;"><FONT SIZE=2>Other
operations represent activities related to the roasting and processing of third-party concentrate and other metals at the Langeloth Facility and
exclude product volumes and costs related to the roasting and processing of TC Mine and Endako Mine concentrate. The Langeloth Facility costs associated with roasting and processing of TC Mine and
Endako Mine concentrate are included in their respective operating results above. </FONT></DD></DL>
 </DIV>
